TWO WATERSPOUTS WERE OBSERVED BY FLORIDA KEYS NWS WEBCAMS SOUTHWEST OF KEY WEST. THE FIRST ONE WAS SKINNY AND ROPE-LIKE WITH A VISIBLE SPRAY RING. THE CONDENSATION CLOUD EXTENDED THREE QUARTERS OF THE WAY FROM CLOUD BASE TO THE SURFACE. THE SECOND ONE WAS WIDER IN APPEARANCE AND GENERALLY MORE VERTICAL, WITH THE CONDENSATION CLOUD EXTENDING ONLY HALFWAY FROM CLOUD BASE TO THE SURFACE. MOVEMENT OF BOTH WATERSPOUTS WAS TO THE WEST-SOUTHWEST AND THEY DISSIPATED BY 421 PM EST.

properties.city: 3 W EAST END OF SEVEN M
properties.remark: REPORT OF A MATURE WATERSPOUT OFF THE SEVEN MILE BRIDGE, NEAR MARATHON, WAS RELAYED VIA BROADCAST MEDIA. THE SKINNY CONDENSATION CLOUD EXTENDED ALMOST THE ENTIRE WAY FROM CLOUD BASE TO THE SURFACE AND A SPRAY RING WAS VISIBLE. IT WAS TILTED AT ABOUT A 45 DEGREE ANGLE MIDWAY UP TO THE CLOUD BASE WHILE MOVING EASTWARD. LOCATION ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR AND SATELLITE IMAGERY. DURATION UNKNOWN.

A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC REPORTED THAT A FAIR WEATHER, NON-SUPERCELLULAR WATERSPOUT MADE LANDFALL ON THE ATLANTIC SIDE OF KEY LARGO IN THE SILVER SHORES COMMUNITY AT 405 PM EDT. A SPRAY RING WAS VISIBLE PRIOR TO LANDFALL. PHOTOS REVEALED DAMAGE TO A DOCK RAILING AND MATURE COCONUT PALM, BUT NO DAMAGE TO NEARBY HOMES WAS OBSERVED. BASED ON ALL AVAILABLE INFORMATION, IT HAS BEEN RATED AN EF-0.

THE CONDENSATION CLOUD EXTENDED ABOUT HALFWAY DOWN FROM THE CLOUD BASE TO THE HORIZON AND STARTED AS A LARGE WEDGE BEFORE TAPERING OFF. VIEW OF THE SURFACE WAS OBSCURED. DURATION WAS APPROXIMATELY 8 MINUTES.

THE WEATHER OBSERVER AT NAVAL AIR STATION KEY WEST ON BOCA CHICA REPORTED A WATERSPOUT SOUTH OF SUGARLOAF KEY AT 354 PM EDT. THE CONDENSATION CLOUD EXTENDED ABOUT HALFWAY DOWN FROM THE CLOUD BASE TO THE HORIZON AND STARTED AS A LARGE WEDGE BEFORE TAPERING OFF. VIEW OF THE SURFACE WAS OBSCURED. DURATION WAS AT LEAST 4 MINUTES, AS THE WATERSPOUT WAS ONGOING AT THE TIME OF THE REPORT.

A FLORIDA KEYS NATIONAL MARINE SANCTUARY TECHNICIAN REPORTED A MATURE WATERSPOUT NORTH OF LAKES PASSAGE, WNW OF KEY WEST. THE WATERSPOUT WAS PENCIL THIN AND NEARLY VERTICAL WITH A SPRAY RING VISIBLE AT THE SURFACE. MOVEMENT WAS SLOWLY NORTHWEST. DURATION WAS APPROXIMATELY 5 MINUTES, DISSIPATING AT 1228 PM EDT. ANOTHER, EXTREMELY NARROW, WATERSPOUT WAS OBSERVED FROM NWS KEY WEST A LITTLER FURTHER NORTH, EXTENDING A QUARTER OF THE WAY FROM CLOUD BASE TO SURFACE. DURATION APPROXIMATELY 3 MINUTES.

A WATERSPOUT WAS OBSERVED VIA NWS KEY WEST ROOFTOP CAMERAS FROM 1256 TO 103 PM EDT. THE CONDENSATION CLOUD EXTENDED ONLY A LITTLE WAY FROM THE CLOUD BASE AND NO SPRAY RING WAS OBSERVED. MOVEMENT WAS SLOWLY NORTHWESTWARD.

A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC REPORTED A BRIEF WATERSPOUT, LESS THAN 1 MINUTE IN DURATION, PASSING THROUGH BOOT KEY HARBOR AT 8:20 AM EDT. IT WAS INDICATED BY A DISTURBED AREA, APPROXIMATELY 50 METERS BY 50 METERS, MOVING THROUGH THE HARBOR AND SPINNING A MOORED BOAT APPROXIMATELY 270 DEGREES. NO CONDENSATION CLOUD WAS OBSERVED.
A NORTH WIND GUST OF 41 KNOTS, OR 47 MPH, WAS RECORDED BY THE WEATHERFLOW MESONET STATION AT CARYSFORT REEF LIGHT AT 503 PM EDT.

UPDATES PREVIOUS WATER SPOUT REPORT FROM 4 SSE KEY WEST FOR MORE TIME/DURATION DETAILS. A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC REPORTED A NARROW, NEARLY VERTICAL WATERSPOUT SSE OF KEY WEST AROUND 5:25 PM EDT. THE CONDENSATION CLOUD EXTENDED ABOUT A THIRD OF THE WAY FROM CLOUD BASE TO THE HORIZON. NO SPRAY RING WAS VISIBLE. MOVEMENT WAS TO THE WEST-NORTHWEST. DURATION WAS APPROXIMATELY 3 MINUTES.

A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC REPORTED A NARROW, NEARLY VERTICAL WATERSPOUT SSE OF KEY WEST AROUND 5:29 PM EDT. THE CONDENSATION CLOUD EXTENDED ABOUT A THIRD OF THE WAY FROM CLOUD BASE TO THE HORIZON. NO SPRAY RING WAS VISIBLE. MOVEMENT WAS TO THE WEST-NORTHWEST. DURATION UNKNOWN.
A WIND GUST OF 39 MPH, OR 34 KNOTS, WAS RECORDED BY THE MESONET SITE AT LOWER SUGARLOAF KEY AT 1254 PM EDT.
A WIND GUST OF 44 MPH, OR 38 KNOTS, WAS RECORDED AT C-MAN STATION SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT AT 250 PM EDT.

CORRECTED PREVIOUS MARINE TSTM WIND REPORT FROM 1 ESE BOCA CHICA TO TROPICAL STORM EVENT TYPE. A WIND GUST OF 40 MPH, OR 35 KNOTS, WAS RECORDED AT THE KEY WEST NAVAL AIR STATION AT BOCA CHICA AT 421 PM EDT. THIS THUNDERSTORM GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A DISTANT RAINBAND OF HURRICANE IAN.
A WIND GUST OF 40 MPH, OR 35 KNOTS, WAS RECORDED AT THE KEY WEST NAVAL AIR STATION AT BOCA CHICA AT 421 PM EDT.

A NWS EMPLOYEE OBSERVED TWO WATERSPOUTS SOUTH OF KEY WEST VIA THE ROOFTOP CAMERA AT 730 PM EDT. BOTH WATERSPOUTS HAD CONDENSATION FUNNELS THAT EXTENDING ONE THIRD OF THE WAY TO THE SURFACE, AND NO SPRAY RINGS WERE OBSERVED FOR EITHER WATERSPOUT. DURATION WAS APPROXIMATELY 10 MINUTES.
SMITH SHOAL LIGHT WEATHERFLOW RECORDED A NNW WIND GUST OF 34 KTS, OR 39 MPH.

A BRIEF, SKINNY WATERSPOUT WAS OBSERVED FROM NWS KEY WEST AT 6:40 PM. IT WAS TILTED AT A NEARLY 45 DEGREE ANGLE AND DRIFTING SLOWLY TO THE WEST. THE CONDENSATION CLOUD EXTENDED A THIRD OF THE WAY FROM CLOUD BASE TO THE HORIZON, BUT VIEW OF THE SURFACE WAS OBSTRUCTED SO NO SPRAY RING WAS VISIBLE. THE WATERSPOUT DISSIPATED AROUND 6:45 PM.

A FAMILY OF 12 WATERSPOUTS WAS OBSERVED FROM 4:58-6:08 PM EDT SOUTH OF THE WESTERNMOST LOWER KEYS. TWO WATERSPOUTS BEGAN ABOUT 6 MILES SOUTH OF BOCA CHICA AND CONTINUED FORMING ALONG A CLOUD LINE THAT EXTENDED SOUTHWESTWARD TO ABOUT 28 MILES SW OF KEY WEST. SPRAY RINGS WERE NOT VISIBLE DUE TO A COMBINATION OF OBSTRUCTED VIEWS OF THE SURFACE AND DISTANCE FROM NWS KEY WEST OFFICE WEBCAMS. THE WIDEST WATERSPOUT FORMED ALONG A KINK IN THE CLOUD LINE WHERE IT SHIFTED MORE WSW. THIS SPOUT LASTED OVER 30 MINUTES AND THE CONDENSATION CLOUD EXTENDED ABOUT A THIRD OF THE WAY FROM THE CLOUD BASE TO THE SURFACE. MOVEMENT WAS TO THE SW AT AROUND 10 KNOTS.

FLORIDA KEYS NWS METEOROLOGISTS AND TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTERS REPORTED TWO WATERSPOUTS IN THE VICINITY OF THE HARBOR KEYS. ONE WAS VERY THIN AND SHORT-LIVED, AND THE OTHER WIDER, SHORT-WEDGE APPEARANCE ONE LASTED APPROXIMATELY 10 MINUTES. THE CONDENSATION CLOUDS EXTENDED ABOUT A QUARTER OF THE WAY DOWN FROM THE CLOUD BASE TO THE HORIZON. VIEW OF THE SURFACE WAS OBSTRUCTED AND SPRAY RINGS WERE NOT VISIBLE. MOVEMENT WAS TO THE SW AT AROUND 10 KNOTS. THESE TWO BRING THE TOTAL NUMBER OF WATERSPOUTS NEAR THE WESTERNMOST LOWER KEYS THIS EVENING TO 14.
CARYSFORT REEF LIGHT WEATHERFLOW RECORDED A WIND GUST OF 39 MPH, OR 34 KNOTS.
